title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: pocketlogR R package logs application events (data jobs, website monitoring, email checks, database checks, etc.) PocketBase instance hosted PocketHost. serves unified operational monitoring system across multiple use cases: ETL pipelines, website uptime checks, data freshness validation, email receipt confirmation, .

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Name: pocketlogR Language: R License: MIT Distribution: GitHub (devtools::install_github()) HTTP backend: httr2 API style: Functional (R6/OOP). exported functions prefixed pl_.

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: package supports two distinct authentication contexts: Admin mode (superuser) — used pl_setup() create collections configure API rules. one-time operation performed PocketBase administrator. User mode (regular user) — used daily operations (pl_log(), pl_get_logs(), etc.). Authenticates regular PocketBase auth collection (default: users), _superusers.

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: PocketBase supports regular user authentication via auth collections. admin creates service account (regular user users collection) dedicated pocketlogR. admin sets API rules pl_flows pl_logs collections authenticated user can list create records. way, R package never needs superuser credentials normal operation. API rule allow authenticated user : @request.auth.id != ""

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: daily usage (regular user): - POCKETLOG_URL — PocketBase instance URL (e.g. https://myapp.pockethost.io) - POCKETLOG_EMAIL — service account email - POCKETLOG_PASSWORD — service account password admin setup (superuser, one-time ): - POCKETLOG_ADMIN_EMAIL — superuser email - POCKETLOG_ADMIN_PASSWORD — superuser password - (Uses POCKETLOG_URL)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Regular user auth: POST /api/collections/users/auth--password Superuser auth: POST /api/collections/_superusers/auth--password return JSON response containing token field (JWT). token must sent Authorization: <token> header subsequent requests.

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Two collections, created admin via pl_setup(conn_admin).

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: API rules (set pl_setup()): - listRule: @request.auth.id != "" - viewRule: @request.auth.id != "" - createRule: @request.auth.id != "" - updateRule: @request.auth.id != "" - deleteRule: null (superuser )

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: API rules (set pl_setup()): - listRule: @request.auth.id != "" - viewRule: @request.auth.id != "" - createRule: @request.auth.id != "" - updateRule: null (superuser — logs immutable) - deleteRule: null (superuser )

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Falls back POCKETLOG_URL, POCKETLOG_EMAIL, POCKETLOG_PASSWORD env vars arguments NULL. Authenticates /api/collections/users/auth--password. Returns connection list: list(url = "...", token = "..."). Errors immediately auth fails (retry). Falls back POCKETLOG_URL, POCKETLOG_ADMIN_EMAIL, POCKETLOG_ADMIN_PASSWORD env vars. Authenticates /api/collections/_superusers/auth--password. Returns connection list structure.
code: # Regular user connection (daily use) pl_connect(url = NULL, email = NULL, password = NULL) # Admin/superuser connection (setup only) pl_connect_admin(url = NULL, email = NULL, password = NULL)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Requires superuser connection pl_connect_admin(). Creates pl_flows pl_logs collections PocketBase don’t exist. Configures API rules authenticated regular users can list/view/create records. Idempotent — safe call multiple times. Called administrator initial setup.
code: pl_setup(conn)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Requires superuser connection pl_connect_admin(). Deletes flow name. PocketBase enforces referential integrity — call fails log entries reference flow. force = TRUE: deletes log entries flow first, deletes flow. safe way fully remove flow. Errors flow found. Requires superuser connection pl_connect_admin(). Deletes log entries matching given filters. arguments optional; combined logic. flow: delete logs flow name. : delete logs created timestamp (POSIXct ISO 8601). status: delete logs status ("SUCCESS", "ERROR", "FATAL"). Called filters, deletes log entries. Returns number deleted records invisibly.
code: pl_delete_flow(conn, flow, force = FALSE) pl_delete_logs(conn, flow = NULL, before = NULL, status = NULL)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: pl_create_flow() registers new flow. Errors flow name already exists. depends_on: optional character vector upstream flow names (e.g. c("ectrl_data_load", "email_confirmation")). Resolved PocketBase record IDs internally. Validates named flows exist adding dependency create cycle DAG. pl_get_flows() returns data.frame flows, optionally filtered type name. depends_on column contains list-column upstream flow names.
code: pl_create_flow(conn, name, type, description = NULL, schedule = NULL, depends_on = NULL) pl_get_flows(conn, type = NULL, name = NULL)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: pl_add_dependency(): adds one upstream dependencies existing flow. depends_on character vector flow names. Validates cycles writing. pl_remove_dependency(): removes one upstream dependencies existing flow. recursive = FALSE: returns direct (immediate) upstream dependencies. recursive = TRUE: walks full DAG upward, returning transitive upstream dependencies.
code: pl_add_dependency(conn, flow, depends_on) pl_remove_dependency(conn, flow, depends_on) pl_get_dependencies(conn, flow, recursive = FALSE)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Returns data.frame representing full dependency chain health given flow. Walks DAG upward (recursively upstream dependencies) collects recent log entry flow chain (including flow ). Columns: flow (name), type, status (latest: SUCCESS/ERROR/FATAL NA never logged), message, created (timestamp latest log), depth (0 = flow , 1 = direct dependency, 2 = dependency dependency, etc.). since: optional POSIXct ISO 8601 string. provided, considers log entries created timestamp determining latest status. Flows logs since time get status = NA. data.frame sorted depth (shallowest first), flow name. function never blocks warns failures — purely informational. caller decides results.
code: pl_get_status(conn, flow, since = NULL)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Returns tibble flows across DAGs (every connected component), raw effective status. flow — flow name type — flow type schedule — schedule string (NA set) raw_status — flow’s latest log status (SUCCESS/ERROR/FATAL/NA never logged) raw_status_time — timestamp latest log (NA never logged) effective_status — SUCCESS, ERROR, FATAL, NA (unknown). flow poisoned (effective status = upstream’s failing status) upstream ERROR/FATAL log recent flow’s last SUCCESS log — meaning downstream ran upstream already broken. downstream never logged, effective_status = NA regardless upstream state. poisoned_by — list-column upstream flow names caused poison. Empty character vector non-poisoned flows. depends_on — list-column direct upstream flow names. is_root — logical, TRUE flow upstream dependencies. since: optional POSIXct ISO 8601 string. provided, log entries timestamp considered determining raw_status. Flows logs window get raw_status = NA. Poisoning rule: downstream poisoned ran upstream already broken (upstream ERROR/FATAL timestamp < downstream last SUCCESS timestamp poisoned; upstream failure timestamp > downstream last SUCCESS timestamp poisoned). downstream never logged, effective_status NA — poisoned. Lives R/deps.R alongside pl_get_status(). Fetch flows via pl_get_flows(). flow, fetch latest log entry (respecting since). flow, walk full upstream DAG recursively; upstream ERROR/FATAL, check failure timestamp recent flow’s raw_status_time — , mark poisoned. effective_status = poisoning upstream’s status poisoned, otherwise raw_status.
code: pl_get_dag(conn, since = NULL)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: pl_create_flow() pl_add_dependency() must validate adding dependency create cycle. validation algorithm: 1. Starting proposed upstream flow(s), walk DAG upward recursively. 2. target flow (one gaining dependency) appears anywhere upstream chain, reject stop() error explaining flows form cycle. 3. simple DFS/BFS reachability check performed R using flow records fetched PocketBase.

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: flow: flow name (character). Resolved PocketBase record ID internally. status: one "SUCCESS", "ERROR", "FATAL". Validated sending. message: optional character string. metadata: optional named list, serialized JSON via jsonlite::toJSON(). Convenience wrappers:
code: pl_log(conn, flow, status, message = NULL, metadata = NULL) pl_success(conn, flow, message = NULL, metadata = NULL) pl_error(conn, flow, message = NULL, metadata = NULL) pl_fatal(conn, flow, message = NULL, metadata = NULL)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Returns data.frame log entries. filter arguments optional; combined logic. / accept POSIXct ISO 8601 character timestamps. limit controls max records returned.
code: pl_get_logs(conn, flow = NULL, status = NULL, from = NULL, to = NULL, limit = 50)

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: HTTP failure: retry 3 times, 2 seconds apart (fixed interval, exponential). retries exhausted: emit R warning() error details return NULL invisibly. Never stop calling script. Fail normally stop() error. interactive/setup operations.

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Default flow types (defined character vector constant utils.R, exported pl_flow_types): data_job — data load / ETL process (e.g. EUROCONTROL data import) website_status — periodic check website expected update (monthly/weekly/yearly) email_check — check expected email received (e.g. data services confirmation) db_check — database freshness check (data date?) website_online — uptime check (website responding?) enforced R level — string accepted type. defaults exist documentation, consistency, convenience.

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Use httr2::request() pipeline style HTTP calls. Use jsonlite JSON serialization/deserialization. Use cli user-facing messages (e.g. pl_setup(), connection success messages). Keep Imports minimal: httr2, jsonlite, cli. exported functions must roxygen2 documentation @export, @param, @return, @examples. Internal helper functions (e.g. resolving flow name ID, building PocketBase filter strings, retry wrapper) exported live R/utils.R. Follow tidyverse style guide (snake_case, . function names except S3 methods). functions accept conn validate proper connection list url token elements.

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Documentation generated pkgdown hosted GitHub Pages. Site URL: https://euctrl-pru.github.io/pocketlogR Config file: _pkgdown.yml — Bootstrap 5 template, reference sections organised function group. Build locally: CI deployment: .github/workflows/pkgdown.yml triggers every push main. uses r-lib/actions/setup-r@v2 r-lib/actions/setup-r-dependencies@v2, calls pkgdown::build_site_github_pages() pushes docs/ gh-pages branch via peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4. Man pages auto-generated roxygen2::roxygenise('.') — never edit .Rd files hand. changing roxygen comments run devtools::document() rebuilding site.

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Framework: testthat (edition 3). Basic tests, HTTP mocking. Test input validation (bad status values, missing required arguments, type checks). Test helper/utility functions (filter string building, metadata serialization, connection object validation). Test retry logic respects 3-attempt limit (can test counter/mock function). Test cycle detection: build mock flow lists verify cycles correctly detected rejected, valid DAGs accepted. Tests must pass without live PocketBase instance.

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: README must contain following sections order:

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Package name: pocketlogR One-line description: “Log application events PocketBase R” Badges: lifecycle (experimental), license (MIT).

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: package : unified operational monitoring via PocketBase/PocketHost. Use cases listed: data job ETL logging, website uptime monitoring, data freshness validation, email receipt confirmation, website update status tracking. Brief explanation uses two PocketBase collections (flows + logs), supports DAG-based flow dependencies chain health checks, authenticates regular user (superuser) daily operations.

what: 3. Admin Setup Guide (for the PocketBase administrator)
title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Step--step instructions screenshots descriptions helpful: Step 1: Create PocketHost instance - Go https://pockethost.io, create account, create new instance. - Note instance URL (e.g. https://myapp.pockethost.io). - Access admin dashboard https://myapp.pockethost.io/_/. Step 2: Create service account user - PocketBase Dashboard, go Collections → users. - Click New record. - Create dedicated user : - Email: e.g. pocketlog-service@yourorg.com - Password: strong password - account R package authenticate . share superuser credentials end users. Step 3: Run initial setup R - Install package (see Installation section). - Set superuser env vars temporarily pass credentials directly. - Run: r   library(pocketlogR)   conn_admin <- pl_connect_admin(     url      = "https://myapp.pockethost.io",     email    = "admin@yourorg.com",     password = "-superuser-password"   )   pl_setup(conn_admin) - creates pl_flows pl_logs collections correct schema API rules. - step, superuser credentials longer needed. Step 4: Verify setup (optional) - Dashboard, go Collections → pl_flows pl_logs. - Click gear icon (collection settings) → API Rules tab. - Confirm: - List, View, Create rules set @request.auth.id != "" - Update Delete rules locked (null) - means authenticated user can read create records, superusers can modify delete . Step 5: Distribute credentials users - Share team: - PocketBase URL - service account email password - Users set environment variables (see User Setup ).

what: 4. User Setup Guide (for R users / data engineers)
title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Environment variables set: Windows (Command Prompt): Note: Restart R/RStudio running setx changes take effect. Windows (PowerShell): Linux / macOS (bash): macOS (zsh): R .Renviron (cross-platform, recommended R users): Edit ~/.Renviron (create project-level .Renviron): Restart R session editing. Use usethis::edit_r_environ() open file easily.
code: setx POCKETLOG_URL "https://myapp.pockethost.io" setx POCKETLOG_EMAIL "pocketlog-service@yourorg.com" setx POCKETLOG_PASSWORD "your-password" [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("POCKETLOG_URL", "https://myapp.pockethost.io", "User") [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("POCKETLOG_EMAIL", "pocketlog-service@yourorg.com", "User") [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("POCKETLOG_PASSWORD", "your-password", "User") echo 'export POCKETLOG_URL="https://myapp.pockethost.io"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export POCKETLOG_EMAIL="pocketlog-service@yourorg.com"' >> ~/.bashrc echo 'export POCKETLOG_PASSWORD="your-password"' >> ~/.bashrc source ~/.bashrc echo 'export POCKETLOG_URL="https://myapp.pockethost.io"' >> ~/.zshrc echo 'export POCKETLOG_EMAIL="pocketlog-service@yourorg.com"' >> ~/.zshrc echo 'export POCKETLOG_PASSWORD="your-password"' >> ~/.zshrc source ~/.zshrc POCKETLOG_URL=https://myapp.pockethost.io POCKETLOG_EMAIL=pocketlog-service@yourorg.com POCKETLOG_PASSWORD=your-password

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("euctrl-pru/pocketlogR")

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Complete working example:
code: library(pocketlogR)  # Connect (reads POCKETLOG_URL, POCKETLOG_EMAIL, POCKETLOG_PASSWORD from env) conn <- pl_connect()  # Register your flows (once) pl_create_flow(conn, "ectrl_data_load", type = "data_job",                description = "Daily EUROCONTROL data import",                schedule = "0 6 * * *")  pl_create_flow(conn, "data_services_email", type = "email_check",                description = "Check data services confirmation email received")  # This flow depends on both the data load AND the email confirmation pl_create_flow(conn, "ans_data_freshness", type = "db_check",                description = "Verify ansperformance.eu datasets are current",                depends_on = c("ectrl_data_load", "data_services_email"))  # Website online check is independent pl_create_flow(conn, "ans_website_online", type = "website_online",                description = "Check ansperformance.eu is reachable")  # Website content update depends on data being fresh AND site being online pl_create_flow(conn, "ans_monthly_update", type = "website_status",                description = "Check ansperformance.eu monthly update",                schedule = "0 8 1 * *",                depends_on = c("ans_data_freshness", "ans_website_online"))  # Add a dependency to an existing flow later pl_add_dependency(conn, "ans_data_freshness", "another_upstream_flow")  # Log outcomes (always works, regardless of upstream status) pl_success(conn, "ectrl_data_load",            message = "Loaded 14,230 rows",            metadata = list(rows = 14230, duration_s = 45.2))  pl_error(conn, "ans_website_online",          message = "HTTP 503 returned",          metadata = list(http_status = 503, response_time_ms = 12040))  # Check the full dependency chain health for a downstream flow status <- pl_get_status(conn, "ans_monthly_update") # Returns a data.frame: #   flow                  type            status   depth #   ans_monthly_update    website_status  SUCCESS  0 #   ans_data_freshness    db_check        SUCCESS  1 #   ans_website_online    website_online  ERROR    1 #   ectrl_data_load       data_job        SUCCESS  2 #   data_services_email   email_check     SUCCESS  2  # Query recent errors errors <- pl_get_logs(conn, status = "ERROR", limit = 20)  # List all website monitoring flows web_flows <- pl_get_flows(conn, type = "website_online")

title: CLAUDE.md — pocketlogR
text: Explain dependency model: flows can declare upstream dependencies via depends_on, forming directed acyclic graph (DAG). Include: - define dependencies creation time add/remove later. - ASCII DAG diagram example showing flow relationships. - pl_get_status() walks chain output looks like. - Note dependencies purely informational — logging never blocked. - Note cycles detected rejected.

text: Equality: field = "value" Comparison: field >= "2024-01-01 00:00:00" : field1 = "value1" && field2 = "value2" Relation filtering: flow.name = "my_flow" filter values must quoted strings PocketBase filter syntax.

title: pocketlogR
text: pocketlogR R package unified operational monitoring. logs events data pipelines, website checks, email confirmations, database freshness checks PocketBase instance hosted PocketHost. Use cases: Data job / ETL pipeline logging Website uptime monitoring Data freshness validation Email receipt confirmation Website update status tracking package writes two PocketBase collections — pl_flows (registry monitored processes) pl_logs (log entries). Flows can declare upstream dependencies, forming DAG lets see cascading failures glance. daily operations, package authenticates regular service account user — superuser credentials needed , initial setup.

title: pocketlogR
text: Flows can declare upstream dependencies creation time later, forming directed acyclic graph (DAG). package validates rejects cycles. Define dependencies creation: Add remove later: pl_get_status() walks full upstream chain returns latest log status flow: pl_get_dag() returns full picture across flows, including cascade (“poisoned”) status — flow marked POISONED ran successfully upstream failure, meaning last result stale: Dependencies purely informational — logging never blocked upstream status.
code: ectrl_data_load ──┐                   ├──► ans_data_freshness ──┐ data_services_email ──┘                    ├──► ans_monthly_update ans_website_online ────────────────────────┘ pl_create_flow(conn, "ans_data_freshness", type = "db_check",                depends_on = c("ectrl_data_load", "data_services_email")) pl_add_dependency(conn, "ans_data_freshness", "another_upstream") pl_remove_dependency(conn, "ans_data_freshness", "another_upstream") pl_get_status(conn, "ans_monthly_update") #>                  flow           type  status depth #>    ans_monthly_update website_status SUCCESS     0 #>    ans_data_freshness       db_check SUCCESS     1 #>    ans_website_online website_online   ERROR     1 #>       ectrl_data_load       data_job SUCCESS     2 #>   data_services_email    email_check SUCCESS     2 dag <- pl_get_dag(conn) #>                  flow           type raw_status effective_status poisoned_by #>       ectrl_data_load       data_job    SUCCESS          SUCCESS          [] #>   data_services_email    email_check    SUCCESS          SUCCESS          [] #>    ans_data_freshness       db_check    SUCCESS         POISONED [ectrl_...] #>    ans_website_online website_online      ERROR            ERROR          [] #>    ans_monthly_update website_status    SUCCESS         POISONED [ans_da...]

title: pocketlogR
text: Five built-types provided consistency. string accepted — enforced.

title: pocketlogR
text: functions require superuser connection (pl_connect_admin()) intended maintenance — day--day use.

title: pocketlogR
text: Flows can deleted superuser. PocketBase enforces referential integrity, log entries flow must removed first. Use force = TRUE one call:
code: conn_admin <- pl_connect_admin()  # Safe delete — removes logs then the flow pl_delete_flow(conn_admin, "old_flow", force = TRUE)  # Without force — errors if logs exist pl_delete_flow(conn_admin, "empty_flow")

title: pocketlogR
code: conn_admin <- pl_connect_admin()  # Delete logs older than 90 days pl_delete_logs(conn_admin, before = Sys.time() - 90 * 86400)  # Delete all error logs for a specific flow pl_delete_logs(conn_admin, flow = "ectrl_data_load", status = "ERROR")  # Delete all logs for a flow (e.g. before removing it) pl_delete_logs(conn_admin, flow = "old_flow")  # Delete everything (use with care) pl_delete_logs(conn_admin)

what: Running Tests Against a Live Database
title: pocketlogR
text: unit tests run without network connection. also run integration tests real PocketBase instance, set additional environment variables running test suite: Add project-level .Renviron root repo (file gitignored): Integration tests skipped automatically variables absent, standard testthat::test_dir() / devtools::test() invocation always works offline.
code: POCKETLOG_URL=https://myapp.pockethost.io POCKETLOG_EMAIL=pocketlog-service@yourorg.com POCKETLOG_PASSWORD=your-password POCKETLOG_ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@yourorg.com POCKETLOG_ADMIN_PASSWORD=your-superuser-password

title: pocketlogR
text: Full function reference articles published pkgdown site GitHub Pages: https://euctrl-pru.github.io/pocketlogR build docs locally: site regenerated automatically every push main via GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/pkgdown.yml.
code: # install.packages("pkgdown") pkgdown::build_site()

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: file intended copied (referenced ) another project’s CLAUDE.md Claude can implement operational logging using pocketlogR. Full docs: https://euctrl-pru.github.io/pocketlogR Source: https://github.com/euctrl-pru/pocketlogR

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: pocketlogR logs application events (ETL jobs, website checks, email confirmations, database freshness checks) shared PocketBase instance. supports DAG-based flow dependencies can see cascading failures across pipelines glance.

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("euctrl-pru/pocketlogR")

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: must set .Renviron system environment. Never hardcode . pl_connect() picks automatically called arguments.

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: Logging functions (pl_success, pl_error, pl_fatal, pl_log) never stop calling script — retry 3 times failure, emit warning return NULL invisibly.
code: library(pocketlogR)  conn <- pl_connect()  # --- your script logic --- tryCatch({   # ... do work ...   pl_success(conn, "my_flow_name",              message = "Completed successfully",              metadata = list(rows = n, duration_s = elapsed)) }, error = function(e) {   pl_error(conn, "my_flow_name", message = conditionMessage(e)) })

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: logging, flow must exist PocketBase. needs done : pl_create_flow() errors flow already exists — safe guard check:
code: conn <- pl_connect() pl_create_flow(conn,                name        = "my_flow_name",                type        = "data_job",       # see Flow Types below                owner       = "quinten",        # required: person or team responsible                description = "What this does",                schedule    = "0 6 * * *")      # optional cron if (nrow(pl_get_flows(conn, name = "my_flow_name")) == 0) {   pl_create_flow(conn, "my_flow_name", type = "data_job") }

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: metadata accepts named R list — serialised JSON automatically:
code: pl_success(conn, flow, message = NULL, metadata = NULL) pl_error(conn,   flow, message = NULL, metadata = NULL) pl_fatal(conn,   flow, message = NULL, metadata = NULL)  # Or directly: pl_log(conn, flow, status = "SUCCESS", message = NULL, metadata = NULL) # status must be one of: "SUCCESS", "ERROR", "FATAL" pl_success(conn, "my_flow", metadata = list(rows = 1200, source = "ECTRL"))

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: string accepted, use standard types consistency:

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: Declare upstream dependencies pl_get_dag() can show cascading failures: Cycles detected rejected automatically.
code: # At creation time: pl_create_flow(conn, "downstream_flow", type = "db_check",                depends_on = c("upstream_flow_a", "upstream_flow_b"))  # Or add later: pl_add_dependency(conn, "downstream_flow", "upstream_flow_a")

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
code: # Full dependency chain health for one flow: pl_get_status(conn, "my_flow") # Returns data.frame: flow, type, status, message, created, depth  # Full DAG across all flows (includes poisoning detection): pl_get_dag(conn) # Returns data.frame with effective_status — a flow is POISONED if an # upstream ERROR/FATAL occurred more recently than its last SUCCESS.  # Query raw log entries: pl_get_logs(conn, flow = "my_flow", status = "ERROR", limit = 20)

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: call pl_setup() — collections already exist PocketBase. call pl_connect_admin() — superuser access needed logging. wrap pl_success / pl_error additional tryCatch — already handle retries internally never throw. log inside tight loop — log per job run (start/end), per row.

title: pocketlogR — Integration Reference for Claude
text: adding project’s CLAUDE.md, append section like:
code: ## pocketlogR context for this project  - Flow name: `"<flow_name>"` (already registered — do not call pl_create_flow) - Type: `"<type>"` - Owner: `"<owner>"` - Schedule: `"<cron or description>"` - Depends on: `c("<upstream_flow>")` (or none) - Log success at the end of the main execution block - Log error in the tryCatch handler with `conditionMessage(e)` as the message - Include relevant metadata: e.g. `list(rows = n, source = "...", duration_s = t)`

what: Add upstream dependencies to a flow — pl_add_dependency

code: pl_add_dependency(conn, flow, depends_on)

title: Add upstream dependencies to a flow — pl_add_dependency
text: conn connection object pl_connect(). flow Name flow update. depends_on Character vector upstream flow names add.

title: Add upstream dependencies to a flow — pl_add_dependency
text: Invisibly returns updated flow record.

title: Add upstream dependencies to a flow — pl_add_dependency
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- pl_connect() pl_add_dependency(conn, "ans_data_freshness", "another_upstream_flow") } # }

what: Connect to PocketBase as a regular user — pl_connect
title: Connect to PocketBase as a regular user — pl_connect
text: Authenticates PocketBase users auth collection returns connection object use daily pocketlogR functions.

title: Connect to PocketBase as a regular user — pl_connect
code: pl_connect(url = NULL, email = NULL, password = NULL)

title: Connect to PocketBase as a regular user — pl_connect
text: url PocketBase instance URL. Defaults POCKETLOG_URL env var. email Service account email. Defaults POCKETLOG_EMAIL env var. password Service account password. Defaults POCKETLOG_PASSWORD env var.

title: Connect to PocketBase as a regular user — pl_connect
text: named list elements url (character) token (character JWT).

title: Connect to PocketBase as a regular user — pl_connect
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- pl_connect() conn <- pl_connect(   url      = "https://myapp.pockethost.io",   email    = "service@example.com",   password = "secret" ) } # }

what: Connect to PocketBase as a superuser (admin) — pl_connect_admin
title: Connect to PocketBase as a superuser (admin) — pl_connect_admin
text: Authenticates _superusers collection. Use pl_setup(). Regular operations use pl_connect().

title: Connect to PocketBase as a superuser (admin) — pl_connect_admin
code: pl_connect_admin(url = NULL, email = NULL, password = NULL)

title: Connect to PocketBase as a superuser (admin) — pl_connect_admin
text: url PocketBase instance URL. Defaults POCKETLOG_URL env var. email Superuser email. Defaults POCKETLOG_ADMIN_EMAIL env var. password Superuser password. Defaults POCKETLOG_ADMIN_PASSWORD env var.

title: Connect to PocketBase as a superuser (admin) — pl_connect_admin
text: named list elements url (character) token (character JWT).

title: Connect to PocketBase as a superuser (admin) — pl_connect_admin
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn_admin <- pl_connect_admin() } # }

title: Create a new flow — pl_create_flow
text: Registers new flow PocketBase. Errors flow name already exists. Validates depends_on flows exist adding create cycle DAG.

title: Create a new flow — pl_create_flow
code: pl_create_flow(   conn,   name,   type,   owner,   description = NULL,   schedule = NULL,   depends_on = NULL )

title: Create a new flow — pl_create_flow
text: conn connection object pl_connect(). name Unique flow identifier (e.g. "ectrl_data_load"). type Flow type string. See pl_flow_types defaults, string accepted. owner Free-text owner responsible party (e.g. "quinten", "team-data"). description Optional human-readable description. schedule Optional cron expression human-readable schedule string. depends_on Optional character vector upstream flow names.

title: Create a new flow — pl_create_flow
text: Invisibly returns created flow record list.

title: Create a new flow — pl_create_flow
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- pl_connect() pl_create_flow(conn, "ectrl_data_load", type = "data_job", owner = "quinten",                description = "Daily EUROCONTROL data import",                schedule = "0 6 * * *") } # }

title: Delete a flow (admin only) — pl_delete_flow
text: Deletes flow name. Requires superuser connection. PocketBase enforces referential integrity, log entries referencing flow must deleted first. Use force = TRUE automatically.

title: Delete a flow (admin only) — pl_delete_flow
code: pl_delete_flow(conn, flow, force = FALSE)

title: Delete a flow (admin only) — pl_delete_flow
text: conn superuser connection object pl_connect_admin(). flow Flow name (character). force TRUE, deletes log entries flow deleting flow . FALSE (default), errors log entries exist.

title: Delete a flow (admin only) — pl_delete_flow
text: Invisibly returns NULL.

title: Delete a flow (admin only) — pl_delete_flow
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn_admin <- pl_connect_admin() pl_delete_flow(conn_admin, "old_flow", force = TRUE) } # }

title: Delete log entries (admin only) — pl_delete_logs
text: Deletes log entries. Requires superuser connection. filter arguments optional combined logic. Called filters, deletes log entries.

title: Delete log entries (admin only) — pl_delete_logs
code: pl_delete_logs(conn, flow = NULL, before = NULL, status = NULL)

title: Delete log entries (admin only) — pl_delete_logs
text: conn superuser connection object pl_connect_admin(). flow Optional flow name. provided, logs flow deleted. Optional POSIXct ISO 8601 string. provided, logs created timestamp deleted. status Optional status string ("SUCCESS", "ERROR", "FATAL"). provided, logs status deleted.

title: Delete log entries (admin only) — pl_delete_logs
text: Invisibly returns number deleted records.

title: Delete log entries (admin only) — pl_delete_logs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn_admin <- pl_connect_admin()  # Delete all logs older than 30 days pl_delete_logs(conn_admin, before = Sys.time() - 30 * 86400)  # Delete all error logs for a specific flow pl_delete_logs(conn_admin, flow = "ectrl_data_load", status = "ERROR")  # Delete all logs for a flow (used internally by pl_delete_flow(force=TRUE)) pl_delete_logs(conn_admin, flow = "old_flow") } # }

title: Default flow types — pl_flow_types
text: character vector default flow type identifiers used pocketlogR. enforced — string accepted type. exist documentation, consistency, convenience.

title: Default flow types — pl_flow_types
code: pl_flow_types

title: Default flow types — pl_flow_types
text: object class character length 5.

what: Get a full DAG overview of all flows and their health — pl_get_dag
title: Get a full DAG overview of all flows and their health — pl_get_dag
text: Returns tibble (data.frame) flows raw (recent log) status effective (cascade-aware) status. flow considered "poisoned" upstream dependency recent ERROR/FATAL log flow's last SUCCESS log — meaning flow ran successfully upstream broke.

title: Get a full DAG overview of all flows and their health — pl_get_dag
code: pl_get_dag(conn, since = NULL)

title: Get a full DAG overview of all flows and their health — pl_get_dag
text: conn connection object pl_connect(). since Optional POSIXct ISO 8601 string. provided, considers log entries created timestamp.

title: Get a full DAG overview of all flows and their health — pl_get_dag
text: data.frame columns: flow (character): flow name type (character): flow type schedule (character): schedule string raw_status (character): recent log status (NA never logged) raw_status_time (character): timestamp recent log effective_status (character): "SUCCESS", "ERROR", "FATAL", "POISONED", NA never logged poisoned_by (list-column): character vector upstream flow names caused poisoning depends_on (list-column): character vector direct upstream flow names is_root (logical): TRUE flow upstream dependencies

title: Get a full DAG overview of all flows and their health — pl_get_dag
text: Poisoning rule: downstream flow poisoned : least one upstream flow ERROR FATAL log. downstream's last SUCCESS log RECENT upstream failure. (.e., downstream ran successfully upstream already broken.) downstream already ERROR FATAL. downstream never logged, effective_status NA (unknown), regardless upstream status.

title: Get a full DAG overview of all flows and their health — pl_get_dag
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- pl_connect() dag <- pl_get_dag(conn) dag } # }

what: Get upstream dependencies of a flow — pl_get_dependencies
title: Get upstream dependencies of a flow — pl_get_dependencies
text: Returns data.frame upstream flows named flow.

title: Get upstream dependencies of a flow — pl_get_dependencies
code: pl_get_dependencies(conn, flow, recursive = FALSE)

title: Get upstream dependencies of a flow — pl_get_dependencies
text: conn connection object pl_connect(). flow Flow name. recursive TRUE, walks full DAG upward returns transitive upstream dependencies. FALSE (default), returns direct (immediate) upstream dependencies.

title: Get upstream dependencies of a flow — pl_get_dependencies
text: data.frame columns: name, type, description, schedule, depth.

title: Get upstream dependencies of a flow — pl_get_dependencies
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- pl_connect() pl_get_dependencies(conn, "ans_monthly_update") pl_get_dependencies(conn, "ans_monthly_update", recursive = TRUE) } # }

title: List flows — pl_get_flows
text: Returns data.frame flows, optionally filtered type name.

title: List flows — pl_get_flows
code: pl_get_flows(conn, type = NULL, name = NULL)

title: List flows — pl_get_flows
text: conn connection object pl_connect(). type Optional flow type filter . name Optional flow name filter .

title: List flows — pl_get_flows
text: data.frame columns: id, name, type, description, schedule, owner, depends_on (list-column upstream flow names), created, updated.

title: List flows — pl_get_flows
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- pl_connect() pl_get_flows(conn) pl_get_flows(conn, type = "data_job") pl_get_flows(conn, name = "ectrl_data_load") } # }

title: Query log entries — pl_get_logs
text: Returns data.frame log entries, optionally filtered. filter arguments optional combined logic.

title: Query log entries — pl_get_logs
code: pl_get_logs(   conn,   flow = NULL,   status = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   limit = 50 )

title: Query log entries — pl_get_logs
text: conn connection object pl_connect(). flow Optional flow name filter . status Optional status filter ("SUCCESS", "ERROR", "FATAL"). Optional start timestamp (POSIXct ISO 8601 string). Optional end timestamp (POSIXct ISO 8601 string). limit Maximum number records return. Default 50.

title: Query log entries — pl_get_logs
text: data.frame columns: id, flow (flow name), status, message, metadata, created.

title: Query log entries — pl_get_logs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- pl_connect() pl_get_logs(conn) pl_get_logs(conn, flow = "ectrl_data_load", status = "ERROR") pl_get_logs(conn, from = Sys.time() - 86400, limit = 100) } # }

what: Get dependency chain health status for a flow — pl_get_status
title: Get dependency chain health status for a flow — pl_get_status
text: Walks DAG upward (recursively upstream dependencies) collects recent log entry flow chain, including flow .

title: Get dependency chain health status for a flow — pl_get_status
code: pl_get_status(conn, flow, since = NULL)

title: Get dependency chain health status for a flow — pl_get_status
text: conn connection object pl_connect(). flow Flow name. since Optional POSIXct ISO 8601 string. provided, log entries created timestamp considered. Flows logs since time get status = NA.

title: Get dependency chain health status for a flow — pl_get_status
text: data.frame sorted depth flow, columns: flow, type, status, message, created, depth.

title: Get dependency chain health status for a flow — pl_get_status
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- pl_connect() pl_get_status(conn, "ans_monthly_update") pl_get_status(conn, "ans_monthly_update", since = Sys.time() - 86400) } # }

what: Log an event for a flow — pl_log
title: Log an event for a flow — pl_log
text: Records log entry named flow. HTTP failure, retries 3 times 2-second intervals. retries fail, emits warning returns NULL invisibly — calling script never stopped.

title: Log an event for a flow — pl_log
code: pl_log(conn, flow, status, message = NULL, metadata = NULL)

title: Log an event for a flow — pl_log
text: conn connection object pl_connect(). flow Flow name (character). status One "SUCCESS", "ERROR", "FATAL". message Optional human-readable log message. metadata Optional named list, serialized JSON.

title: Log an event for a flow — pl_log
text: Invisibly returns created log record, NULL failure.

title: Log an event for a flow — pl_log
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ conn <- pl_connect() pl_log(conn, "ectrl_data_load", "SUCCESS",        message = "Loaded 14230 rows",        metadata = list(rows = 14230, duration_s = 45.2)) } # }
